title: Higher Education vs Vocational Training
passage:
Higher Education and Vocational Training

Higher education is important for many students. It includes universities and colleges. Students learn many subjects in higher education. They can study for a degree. A degree helps students get better jobs. Many students want to go to university after high school.

On the other hand, vocational training is different. It teaches specific skills for jobs. Students learn how to do a job well. For example, they can learn to be a chef or a mechanic. Vocational training is often shorter than higher education. It can take one or two years.

Both options are good. Higher education can be more expensive. However, vocational training is less expensive. Some students choose vocational training because they want to start working quickly.

In conclusion, students should think about what they want to do. Higher education is good for some jobs. Vocational training is good for other jobs. Each path has its benefits.

questions.instruction: Choose the correct letter, A, B, C or D.
questions.text: What do students learn in higher education?
questions.explanation: In the passage, it states that students learn many subjects in higher education.

questions.instruction: Choose the correct letter, A, B, C or D.
questions.text: How long does vocational training usually take?
questions.explanation: The passage mentions that vocational training often takes one or two years.
The passage states that higher education can be more expensive.
The passage states that vocational training teaches specific skills for jobs.

questions.text: What is one job mentioned in vocational training?
questions.explanation: The passage gives the example of learning to be a chef.

vocabulary.meaning: A qualification given to students after they complete their studies.
Related to training for a specific job.
A person who repairs cars and other machines.
